title: Flux rigidity for ancient single-mode solutions of the two-dimensional hydrostatic Euler equations — paper and verification package
description:
Paper (18 pp) and gated exact-verification package for Flux rigidity for ancient single-mode solutions of the two-dimensional hydrostatic Euler equations.

The paper proves a Liouville-type flux-rigidity theorem for the 2D hydrostatic Euler equations on the periodic cell: for each harmonic k ≥ 1, every exact single-mode solution which is ancient, has uniformly bounded transverse velocity, and none of whose regular stream-function level components is freely homotopic to a horizontal circle, has vanishing backward Cesàro mean flux.

This archive contains the LaTeX source and compiled PDF together with four standalone Python/SymPy verification scripts (exact classification and flux identities with symbolic k; a gated float64 check of the counterexample with no hand-coded derivatives; a nine-mutation corruption battery), a Makefile with verify/verify-fast/verify-mutations targets, pinned dependency versions, and chained SHA-256 checksums. None of the scripts is load-bearing for the proofs, which are complete in the paper; they pin the computational identities and make the counterexample independently checkable. The manuscript underwent twenty-two rounds of independent review, including cross-model audits.
